title: ibervillea macdougalii
intro: Ibervillea macdougalii is a type of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the nightshade family. This plant is also known as the macdougal's nightshade or devil's head, and it has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The roots of Ibervillea macdougalii contain solanine, a compound that has been used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or poultice, and its herbal preparations have been used to relieve pain, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing.
